title: quercus catesbaei f. rappii
intro: Quercus catesbaei f. rappii, also known as the Rapp's oak, is a variety of the Catesby oak tree, a type of evergreen tree native to the southeastern United States. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that can help soothe skin irritations and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Quercus catesbaei f. rappii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Quercus catesbaei f. rappii is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
